extraction.one_line_summary: GANNA Filippo won the 34.1km Valdobbiadene time trial by 26 seconds over DENNIS Rohan, while ALMEIDA João extended his Maglia Rosa lead to 56 seconds.
tactical_patterns: MCNULTY Brandon jumped from 11th to 4th GC with only 1:09 deficit to stage winner, exploiting time trial to vault into contention.
NIBALI Vincenzo showed uneven pacing: weak start, strong middle, weak finish pattern suggests declining TT endurance in Grand Tours.
ALMEIDA João gained time bonuses on Stage 13 before TT, using intermediate days to pad GC buffer before weak discipline.

rider_signals.GANNA Filippo: positive: 17 seconds faster than DENNIS at final time check and 26 seconds at finish shows accelerating power through long TTs.
rider_signals.DENNIS Rohan: positive: First road win since 2019 Harrogate Worlds confirms return to peak TT form after extended slump.
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: Only 6 seconds slower than GANNA at first time check reveals explosive opening ability rare for GC riders.
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: positive: 6th on stage while extending GC lead to 56 seconds proves adequate TT form defies expectations for climber debut.
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: neutral: Stayed 2nd GC but lost time to ALMEIDA despite no TT win in 5 years suggests chronic inability to challenge leaders.
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: neutral: Held 3rd but slipped to 2:11 down reveals time trialing as clear ceiling to GC ambitions.
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: Erratic pacing profile indicates age-related decline in sustained TT power management.
